diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers gave the same unsupported label, cited no evidence, and used the same high confidence. The only substantive difference is the reason-code choice. Option 1 uses the more specific code 'central_proposition_unsupported,' directly indicating that the claim itself is not supported by the evidence bundle. Option 2 uses the broader code 'insufficient_evidence,' which also fits because no evidence item establishes the claimed preference. The rationales are materially aligned: both say the evidence does not support 'prefer_supported_stack.' This is best diagnosed as a granularity difference rather than a disagreement about evidence or interpretation.
